title: Feed Discovery, WHATWG URL Identity, Redirects, and SSRF · Narrowing Workshop
summary: A disability-led research network incrementally builds authorized TypeScript discovery policy from original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and browser fixtures while retaining prior TypeScript, Node, HTTP-client, Express, SQL, Git, accessibility, security, testing, async-cleanup, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of authorized TypeScript discovery policy while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory,…
objectives: Distinguish direct feeds from bounded HTML autodiscovery and require explicit source intent and supported media evidence.
Admit supported alternate RSS or Atom links, resolve against the final document URL, deduplicate, rank transparently, and bound candidates.
Use WHATWG URL parsing and a documented equivalence policy without collapsing query, case, port, path, fragment, or percent-encoding distinctions unsafely.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Ts Direct Discovery Mode
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop TypeScript team handles case 2180 during a cross-tenant privacy and cache audit. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to reconcile direct and autodiscovery, URL identity, redirects, SSRF, and provenance; publisher fetch and retention budgets must remain explicit; then inject one URL, DNS, redirect, stream, compression, timeout, abort, or publisher-budget failure and settle ownership. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Ts Direct Discovery Mode.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for distinguish direct feeds from bounded html autodiscovery and require explicit source intent and supported media evidence. For Feed aggregation and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Ts Direct Discovery Mode, keep Feed Ts Direct Discovery Mode connected to executable TypeScript unknown-validation, async, PostgreSQL, Express, reader, package, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.
